Examples

Here are two of my recent papers formatted with Quarto:

  • [1] is a tutorial paper on technical writing with Quarto.
  • [2] is a full technical paper, whose source is available on Gitlab.

Note that there is now an official extension to format the output according to IEEE template

Introduction

Context

  • Big-picture challenge in the world (“hook the audience”).
  • Zoom into a specific topic.
  • Prior knowledge & work, scientific SoA
  • Open research question & gap
    • there is a need to address this gap

Objectives / Sensor Rqts

  • High-level restrictions (IN vs OUT of scope)
  • Specific measurable metrics

Preview

  • Preview the novel idea (concept, approach, …) TIP: include a concept overview figure on page 1. See Figure 1.
  • Highlight unique contributions.
  • Preview paper structure: Section 1 describes …

(Related Works)

In long paper, dedicated section to review the state of the art.

Design

“Methods” if more appropriate. Use action verb to emphasize the procedure (the “recipe”).

Figure 2 shows the architecture block diagram.

Design of element 1

Design of element 2

Implementation

Pictures of realizations and/or experimental setups.

(Experimental) results

  • Test 1
  • Test 2

Discussion

  • Key insights
  • Comparison with previous work
  • Highlight the strengths by explaining
    • how the paper moves the SoA
    • how the paper addresses the original objectives
  • Discuss limitations and recommendation for future work

Conclusion

  • Summary: methods & results & significance
  • Extrapolate / speculate on the broad impact

Refresher on XXX

It might be needed to provide a refresher on some well-known background knowledge. Review of Theory X, Fundamentals of X, …

Nomenclature / notation

List of symbols. Sometimes included just after the intro.

References

[1]
G. Close, “Technical Writing and Publishing Data-Rich Articles with Quarto,” Sep. 22, 2022. [Online]. Available: https://gael-close.github.io/2209-tech-writing.html
[2]
B. Brajon, E. Gasparin, and G. Close, “A benchmark of integrated magnetometers and magnetic gradiometers,” IEEE Access, vol. 11, pp. 115635–115643, 2023, doi: 10.1109/ACCESS.2023.3325035.
